question: What two functions does the Temporal 'Flux-Anchor' device serve?

answers.original: The Temporal Flux-Anchor creates Time-Locks to prevent temporal manipulation in localized areas and generates Chronos-Pulses to manipulate time flow.
answers.perturbed: The Temporal Flux-Anchor creates Time-Locks to prevent temporal manipulation in localized areas and is essential for maintaining temporal stability.

ops.description: The production of Umbradium results in the creation of a localized zone that disrupts kinetic energy transfer and dampens electromagnetic fields.<SEP>The production of Umbradium creates a localized zone that disrupts kinetic energy transfer and dampens electromagnetic fields.

entities.name: Temporal Flux-Anchor
entities.description: The Temporal Flux-Anchor is a device capable of creating Time-Locks and generating Chronos-Pulses to manipulate time flow.

entities.name: Flux-Anchor Technology
entities.description: Flux-Anchor technology is highly classified and used for creating Time-Locks and generating Chronos-Pulses.

entities.name: Temporal Stability
entities.description: Temporal stability refers to the maintenance of consistent time flow, essential for temporal society.

entities.name: Chronos-Pulse
entities.description: A Chronos-Pulse is a temporal effect that can slow or accelerate time flow, appearing as rippling distortions in the air.

relations.description: Flux-Anchors are essential for maintaining temporal stability.
The Temporal Flux-Anchor creates Time-Locks to prevent temporal manipulation in localized areas.
